title: Macro Matters: Stanford’s Duffie on Fed Balance-Sheet Limits
summary: Darrell Duffie, the Adams Distinguished Professor of Management and Finance at Stanford Graduate School of Business, joins Bloomberg Intelligence’s Ira Jersey on this Macro Matters edition of the FICC Focus podcast to discuss how much the Federal Reserve can realistically shrink its balance sheet under Chair Kevin Warsh. Duffie argues that the real constraint isn’t on the asset side, but on the liability side of the Fed’s balance sheet, especially reserve balances, paper currency and the Treasur

summary: A Ukrainian intelligence officer, who previously admitted to killing a woman linked to a bombing in Monaco, retracted his confession and shifted the blame to another defendant as their bail hearing opened in Kyiv.

title: SK Hynix Said to Guide US Offering Price 3.1% Above Korea Close
summary: SK Hynix Inc. is telling investors it intends to price its US listing at $149 per American depositary receipt, according to people familiar with the matter, despite recent volatility in the Korean memory chipmaker’s shares.

title: A New Challenge for an African Oil Giant Is Building Trust
summary: The way Nigeria’s president is going about reforms is beginning to worry investors.

title: Brussels Embraces ‘Made in Europe’ Drive to Curb Chinese Dependency
summary: National governments should favor European suppliers in public procurement, according to draft document

title: Luxury Car Firm DuPont Registry Group to Pick Banks for IPO
summary: DuPont Registry Group, a luxury car marketplace and media company, has picked banks for a US initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Pricey Credit Markets Fuel Anything Funds
summary: Bloomberg's Tasos Vossos joins Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg Real Yield." Bond investors are increasingly betting on flexible funds that can buy whatever they like as lofty credit market valuations leave little margin for error. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Kimmeridge Faults Pace of Devon Energy’s Asset-Sale Efforts
summary: Kimmeridge Energy Management Co., an outspoken shale investor, criticized Devon Energy Corp.’s divestment program as too slow after the driller’s $25 billion takeover of Coterra Energy Inc.

title: Spain’s Joint Debt Proposal Queried by Largest EU Economies
summary: A Spanish bid to boost joint debt issuance by the European Union was met with skepticism from some of the bloc’s top economies.

title: New Zealand Dollar Surges as Economic Data Fuels Rate Hike Bets
summary: The New Zealand dollar advanced Thursday to the highest in more than two weeks after strong manufacturing data increased bets on further monetary tightening in the nation.

title: US 30-Year Bond Auction Draws Highest Yield Since 2007
summary: An auction of 30-year Treasury bonds Thursday drew the highest yield in nearly 20 years, underscoring how swelling bond supply is driving investors to demand higher returns from government debt.

title: Stocks Get Boost From Chipmakers as Oil Declines: Markets Wrap
summary: A resurgence in giant chipmakers powered a rebound on Wall Street, with stocks also rising amid a decline in oil prices as traders looked past fresh hostilities between the US and Iran.

title: Oil Industry Retained Up to €200 Million of German Fuel Subsidy
summary: Germany’s oil industry retained between €100 million and €200 million from a fuel tax reduction earlier this year that was intended to ease the impact of surging costs on motorists, a government advisory panel found.

title: Chip Stocks Rally on AI Boom | Open Interest 7/9/2026
summary: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." Chipmakers are powering stocks higher as SK Hynix prepares for its US debut. Pepsi flashes retail warning signs as shoppers pull back. And tensions rise in the Middle East after fresh US-Iran strikes. Plus, Kurt Wagner on Zuckerberg’s AI monetization push, Flexport CEO Ryan Petersen on global shipping risks, and Dan Suzuki on why Fed cuts may beat hikes. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Repricing of 30-Year Treasury Yield Has Just Begun, PGIM’s Peters Says
summary: The return of the 30-year US Treasury yield above 5% is prompting investors to rethink what they consider an attractive level, according to PGIM’s Gregory Peters.

title: CFTC Blocks for Now CME’s Plan to Offer 24/7 Oil Futures
summary: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission has blocked for now CME Group Inc.’s bid to start offering round-the-clock trading in oil futures.

title: Franklin’s Dudley Sees AI Infrastructure Bull Case Through 2027
summary: Franklin Templeton’s Katrina Dudley said the bull case for infrastructure spending on artificial intelligence is winning out over bear arguments and sees the investment theme remaining durable through 2027 and potentially into the year beyond.

title: ING Weighs Venezuela Commodity Deals as Client Requests Rise
summary: ING Groep, one of the top financiers of commodity trading, is fielding “a lot” of calls from clients to support Venezuelan natural resource deals as the country revamps exports after years of US sanctions, its new Americas commodity finance head said.

title: Blackstone Data-Center Firm QTS Taps Leveraged Loans
summary: A unit of Blackstone-backed QTS Realty Trust LLC is seeking to raise $2 billion to repay debt tied to US data centers and fund a distribution.

title: Why Fed Cuts Beat Rate Hikes
summary: Markets may be pricing yesterday's economy. iCapital's Dan Suzuki joined Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why slowing job growth and cooling inflation make Fed rate cuts more likely than hikes, why higher Treasury yields haven't broken the stock rally, and why AI leadership may be shifting away from chipmakers toward hyperscalers that can actually monetize their massive investments. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Wall Street Finds a New Way to Help Investors Avoid Elon Musk
summary: For every investor who wants more Elon Musk, Wall Street increasingly has an ETF. Now it’s planning some for investors who want less.

title: A New Texas Mayor Takes Over Amid 2,200% Tax-Base Boom
summary: Mark Hill, the new mayor of Frisco, Texas, talks about the challenges and opportunities of managing a city whose population has soared soared 600% and whose tax base is up 2,200% in the past 25 years. He speaks with Bloomberg's Julie Fine. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: UK Fintech Lendable Pledges Personal Loans to Raise £500 Million
summary: UK digital lender Lendable Ltd. is raising £500 million ($671 million) by selling securities backed by a portfolio of personal loans, a deal that would support its ambitions to push further into lending.

title: Most Emerging Market Currencies Rebound Amid ‘Headline Fatigue’
summary: Most emerging-market currencies are rebounding on Thursday from losses the day before as oil edges lower, with investors playing down the scope of resumed hostilities between the US and Iran.

title: Corn, Soy Ease Ahead of WASDE as Traders Eye Relief From Heat
summary: Corn and soybean futures were easing on pressure from an improving US weather outlook as traders looked ahead to the monthly WASDE supply and demand outlook due Friday.

title: Nigerian Stocks Vault Past Korea’s Kospi to Claim World’s Top Returns
summary: Nigerian equities have overtaken South Korea’s to hand investors the highest dollar-based returns this year, as souring sentiment on artificial-intelligence stocks pushes the Asian nation’s world-beating rally into bear territory.

title: Tech Drives US Stocks Higher as Traders Downplay Iran War Risks
summary: US stocks rose as investors put aside concerns over the latest flare-up between the US and Iran to pile on technology stocks after recent declines spurred dip buying.

title: Hormuz Shipping Grinds to Near Halt After US, Iran Strikes
summary: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz came to a near standstill after the US struck Iran for a second straight day, leaving a fragile truce between the two sides looking increasingly shaky.

title: Airbus Employees in Spain Go on Strike Over Working Conditions
summary: Airbus SE employees at a major manufacturing site in Spain have gone on strike in protest of what they say are deteriorating work conditions over the past years.

title: Trump Stops in UK to Switch Air Force One Planes
summary:
President Trump’s return from the NATO summit in Turkey took a detour when he switched Air Force One planes in the UK.

Annmarie Hordern explains why the new Qatari-donated jet is raising security questions. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: European Gas Prices Jump as Qatar Pauses Push to Ramp Up LNG
summary: European natural gas prices jumped above €50 a megawatt-hour for the first time in a month after a report that Qatar is pausing efforts to rapidly revive production at the world’s largest liquefied natural gas facility, threatening a recovery of flows through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Oil Edges Lower as Traders Assess Renewed US-Iran Hostilities
summary:
Oil edged lower as traders assessed renewed US strikes against Iran, with the limited scope of the hostilities prompting bets that energy shipments through the Persian Gulf will resume their recent recovery.
Leslie Palti-Guzman, founder at Energy Vista, discusses the outlook for energy as tensions ramp up in the Middle East. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: UBS Strategists Turn Biggest European Stock Bulls With 8% Upside
summary: Stronger earnings and a broadening rally will power European stocks to new highs this year, according to UBS Group AG strategists, who are now the biggest bulls among forecasters tracked by Bloomberg.

title: Africa CDC Urges Congo to Pay Striking Ebola Workers
summary: Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention is working with the Democratic Republic of Congo to speed up overdue payments to Ebola responders after strikes in the epidemic’s epicenter threatened efforts to contain what officials say is the world’s fastest-growing Ebola outbreak.

title: Dronemaker Helsing Alters Staff Stock Plan Before Funding Round
summary: European defense startup Helsing has overhauled its internal share program, alienating some staff as the company finalizes its biggest funding round to date.
